posts.title: Pressing triggers — 3-zone drill
posts.body:
## Setup

- Full pitch split into 3 zones
- Full squad
- Duration: 30 min

## How it works

Team A builds from the back. Team B presses. Each zone has a specific rule:

**Zone 1 (defensive third):** No pressure — let them build

**Zone 2 (middle third):** Press on trigger (back pass or GK touch)

**Zone 3 (attacking third):** Full press immediately on every touch

## Key coaching points

- Press in pairs — never alone
- After pressing, recover shape quickly
- Communication — call the press out loud

## Progressions

1. Walk through the zones first
2. Add light resistance
3. Full intensity — game realistic

posts.title: Finishing under pressure — 1v1 with keeper
posts.body:
## Setup

- Half-pitch
- Full squad in pairs
- Duration: 25 min

## How it works

Player A and Player B do a quick 1-2 combination on the edge of the box. After the combination, Player A receives the ball and finishes 1v1 with the keeper. Player B applies light pressure from behind.

## Key coaching points

- First touch should set up the shot — not take you away from goal
- Decide early: near post or far post
- Stay composed — don't rush the finish

## Variations

- Add a passive defender at the edge of the area
- Vary the starting position (left side, right side, central)

posts.title: Rondo 4v2 — ball control & awareness
posts.body:
## Setup

- Small square (10x10m)
- 6 players minimum, 12 maximum
- Duration: 20 min

## Rules

4 players keep the ball, 2 players press in the middle.

- One-touch passing only (or two-touch for beginners)
- If the 2 win the ball, the player who lost it goes into the middle
- Winner stays — keep track of who avoids going in

## Key coaching points

- Constant movement — don't stand still after passing
- Open body shape to see all options before receiving
- Quick decision-making — the ball should move faster than the defenders

## Progressions

1. Two-touch allowed
2. One-touch only
3. Add a neutral player who always plays with the team in possession (5v2)
